info.name: tagslut-api
info.description:
## Folder map
- **TIDAL**: TIDAL v2 track fetch + album/playlist track expansion.
- **Beatport**: Beatport track fetch + hydrate/search fallbacks.
- **Comparison & Normalization**: Provider JSON -> canonical parsing/validation and TIDAL vs Beatport comparison/status.
- **Operator Output**: Human-readable summary / print outputs.
- **Utilities**: Auth/token setup and misc helpers.

exec: // Enumerates track candidates from a TIDAL album (JSON:API).
// JSON:API: top-level must have data array
pm.test('FAIL: No data array — cannot enumerate album tracks', () => {
pm.expect.fail('JSON:API data array missing. Schema drift or wrong endpoint.');
// Build included map for attribute lookup (JSON:API includes)
// rel is a relationship item: {type, id} or {type, id, attributes}
// Try to get attributes from included
pm.expect(count).to.be.above(0, 'No tracks found in album. Check lookup_album_id_tidal.');
console.log(`[TIDAL ALBUM] ${count} track candidate(s) stored in tidal_album_track_candidates`);
console.log('[TIDAL ALBUM] To validate a candidate: set lookup_isrc and lookup_track_id_tidal from the list, then run the primary ISRC fetch.');
// Print first few candidates for operator inspection
console.log(`  [${i}] id=${c.id} | isrc=${c.isrc || '(no isrc in relationship)'} | title=${c.title || '(no title in relationship)'}`);

request.description: SECONDARY — TIDAL album expansion. Enumerates all track candidates from a TIDAL album. Stores results in tidal_album_track_candidates (JSON array of {id, isrc, title}) and sets tidal_candidate_count. Does NOT perform identity join — use the primary ISRC fetch to validate individual candidates by setting lookup_isrc and lookup_track_id_tidal from the candidates list.

exec: pm.test('TIDAL response is valid JSON', () => { throw new Error(e.message); });
pm.environment.set('tidal_status', 'FAIL: Schema drift — no data key');
pm.test('TIDAL JSON:API structure', () => { throw new Error('Missing body.data'); });
// ─── Fetch - TIDAL v2 Track ─── pre-request script ───────────────────────────
// Guards against missing/expired TIDAL bearer token.
// If tidal_refresh_token + tidal_refresh_url are present, auto-refreshes.
// Otherwise fails loudly with operator instructions.
// ── Attempt refresh if credentials are available ──────────────────────────
const canRefreshWithRefreshToken = refreshTok && refreshTok.trim() !== "" && refreshUrl && refreshUrl.trim() !== "";
const canRefreshWithClientCreds  = clientId && clientId.trim() !== "" && clientSec && clientSec.trim() !== "" && refreshUrl && refreshUrl.trim() !== "";
console.log("[TIDAL Pre] Attempting token refresh via refresh_token grant...");
console.error("[TIDAL Pre] Refresh request failed:", err);
throw new Error("[TIDAL Pre] OPERATOR ACTION REQUIRED: Token refresh failed. Run 'Auth - TIDAL (Set access token)' manually.");
throw new Error("[TIDAL Pre] Refresh response not JSON. Status: " + res.status);
throw new Error("[TIDAL Pre] Refresh response missing access_token: " + JSON.stringify(json));
console.log("[TIDAL Pre] Token refreshed successfully via refresh_token.");
console.log("[TIDAL Pre] Attempting token refresh via client_credentials grant...");
console.error("[TIDAL Pre] Client-creds refresh failed:", err);
throw new Error("[TIDAL Pre] OPERATOR ACTION REQUIRED: Token refresh failed. Run 'Auth - TIDAL (Set access token)' manually.");
throw new Error("[TIDAL Pre] Refresh response not JSON. Status: " + res.status);
throw new Error("[TIDAL Pre] Refresh response missing access_token: " + JSON.stringify(json));
console.log("[TIDAL Pre] Token refreshed successfully via client_credentials.");
// No refresh path available — fail loudly
"[TIDAL Pre] OPERATOR ACTION REQUIRED: tidal_access_token is " + reason +
" and no refresh credentials are configured. " +
"Run 'Auth - TIDAL (Set access token)' first, or set tidal_client_id + tidal_client_secret in the environment."

query.description: ISRC lookup — set lookup_isrc in environment

request.description:
Fetch a TIDAL v2 track via JSON:API. Uses env vars:
- baseUrl_tidal
- tidal_track_id
- tidal_country_code
- tidal_include
- tidal_access_token

Saves raw provider response into `raw_tidal_track`.

exec: // Only run when lookup_track_id_beatport is already set by
// the primary ISRC list fetch. Validates single-track response.
// Guard: must be a single object, not a list
pm.test('Response is a single track object (not a list)', () => {
// Cross-check: ID must match what was set by primary fetch
// Enrich beatport vars with hydrated data
console.log('[BP HYDRATE] Track hydrated:', body.name, '| ISRC:', body.isrc);
pm.test('FAIL: Schema drift — required fields missing in hydrate response', () => {
pm.expect.fail('Schema drift: id/isrc/name missing. Do not proceed.');

request.description: SECONDARY — Beatport track hydration by ID. Use only when lookup_track_id_beatport is already known (set by the primary ISRC list fetch). Returns the full single-track object for deeper field inspection. Do not use this as a primary lookup — it bypasses ISRC validation.

exec: // Extracts all beatport_* environment variables from the Beatport API response.
//   Shape A: Single track object at root (GET /catalog/tracks/{id})
// Fails loudly if response shape is unrecognised.
pm.test("Beatport response is valid JSON", function () {
pm.expect.fail("Response is not valid JSON: " + e.message);
console.warn("[Beatport Parse] Multiple results (" + body.results.length + "). Using first. IDs: " +
console.warn("[Beatport Parse] Empty results array. ISRC may not be in Beatport catalog.");
pm.expect.fail("No results returned from Beatport. Track not in catalog.");
"[Beatport Parse] UNKNOWN RESPONSE SHAPE. Cannot extract track data. " +
"Expected: root track object, { results: [...] }, or { track: {...} }."
console.error("[Beatport Parse] Unknown shape. Body sample:", JSON.stringify(body).substring(0, 500));
pm.expect.fail("[Beatport Parse] Response is not an object. Got: " + typeof body);
const bpm = track.bpm !== undefined && track.bpm !== null ? track.bpm : null;
// Beatport key can be: { name: "A min", camelot: "8A" } or a string
// Beatport returns duration in seconds (integer) or as "mm:ss" string
genreValue = track.genres.map(function (g) { return typeof g === "object" ? g.name : g; }).join(", ");
subGenreValue = track.sub_genres.map(function (g) { return typeof g === "object" ? g.name : g; }).join(", ");
return { id: a.id || null, name: a.name || null, slug: a.slug || null };
return { id: r.id || null, name: r.name || null };
// Beatport: track.image.uri or track.image.dynamic_uri or release.image.uri
// ── 14. Store raw track for downstream use ────────────────────────────────
// Auto-refreshes Beatport access token if missing or expired.
// Uses beatport_refresh_token if available, otherwise falls back to
console.warn("[Beatport Pre] Token is " + reason + ". Attempting refresh...");
"[Beatport Pre] OPERATOR ACTION REQUIRED: beatport_access_token is " + reason +
"Set beatport_username + beatport_password (or beatport_client_id + beatport_client_secret) in the environment, " +
throw new Error("[Beatport Pre] Token refresh request failed: " + err);
throw new Error("[Beatport Pre] Token refresh response not JSON. Status: " + res.status + " Body: " + res.text());
throw new Error("[Beatport Pre] Token refresh response missing access_token. Body: " + JSON.stringify(json));
console.log("[Beatport Pre] Token refreshed successfully via", grantType);

request.description:
Fetch a Beatport track (endpoint may differ depending on your Beatport API plan).

Uses env vars:
- baseUrl_beatport
- beatport_track_id
- beatport_access_token

Saves raw provider response into `raw_beatport_track`.

exec: // Clear any stale beatport canonical vars
pm.test('Beatport ISRC lookup: unambiguous single match', () => { throw new Error(reason); });
failLoud('Response is not valid JSON: ' + e.message);
failLoud('Response body is not an object');
// Beatport v4 list responses use { results: [...], count: N, next, previous }
failLoud('Schema drift: expected body.results to be an array, got: ' + typeof results);
failLoud('No tracks found for ISRC ' + pm.environment.get('lookup_isrc'), 0);
failLoud('Ambiguous: ' + count + ' tracks returned for ISRC ' + pm.environment.get('lookup_isrc') + ' — cannot determine canonical match', count);
const missing = REQUIRED_FIELDS.filter(f => track[f] === undefined || track[f] === null);
failLoud('Schema drift: missing required fields: ' + missing.join(', '), 1);
// Validate ISRC matches what we looked up
failLoud('ISRC mismatch: requested ' + lookupIsrc + ' but got ' + isrc, 1);
pm.test('Beatport ISRC lookup: unambiguous single match', () => { pm.expect(count).to.equal(1); });
pm.test('Beatport track has required fields', () => { pm.expect(missing.length).to.equal(0); });

request.description:
Parses `raw_beatport_track` into canonical fields and provenance.

Writes/merges:
- canonical_track (adds beatport-side fields without removing tidal-side)
- provenance_map

Fails loudly on missing/invalid JSON or major schema drift.

exec: // JSON:API parsing only — no fallback to non-JSON:API shapes
pm.test('TIDAL JSON:API parse: valid canonical record', () => { throw new Error(reason); });
failLoud('Response is not valid JSON: ' + e.message);
failLoud('Schema drift: missing body.data (not a JSON:API response)');
// data may be array (search) or single object (by-id)
failLoud('No TIDAL track data returned for ISRC ' + pm.environment.get('lookup_isrc'));
// For ISRC lookup, expect exactly one track
failLoud('Ambiguous: ' + dataArr.length + ' TIDAL tracks returned — cannot determine canonical match');
// JSON:API resource object: { id, type, attributes, relationships }
failLoud('Schema drift: JSON:API resource missing attributes');
const missingAttrs = REQUIRED_ATTRS.filter(f => attrs[f] === undefined || attrs[f] === null || attrs[f] === '');
failLoud('Schema drift: missing required attributes: ' + missingAttrs.join(', '));
const inc = included.find(i => i.type === ref.type && i.id === ref.id);
return inc && inc.attributes && inc.attributes.name ? inc.attributes.name : null;
const incAlbum = included.find(i => i.type === albumRef.type && i.id === albumRef.id);
failLoud('ISRC mismatch: requested ' + lookupIsrc + ' but got ' + isrc);
pm.test('TIDAL JSON:API parse: valid canonical record', () => { pm.expect(missingAttrs.length).to.equal(0); });

request.description:
Parses `raw_tidal_track` (JSON:API) into canonical fields and a provenance map.

Writes:
- canonical_track (JSON string; tidal-side only fields populated)
- provenance_map

Fails loudly on JSON:API schema drift / missing expected keys.

exec: // Reflects workflow class and candidate counts
{ field: 'Track ID',          tidal: tidalTrackId,    beatport: bpTrackId },
{ field: 'BP match count',    tidal: '—',             beatport: bpMatchCount },
{ field: 'Comparison notes',  tidal: operatorNotes,   beatport: operatorNotes },
th { background: #1a1a2e; color: #e0e0e0; padding: 6px 10px; text-align: left; }
td { padding: 5px 10px; border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd; vertical-align: top; }

request.description: Final step for runner: prints canonical/provenance/comparison/summary to the Postman console and leaves them in environment vars.

exec: // ─── Auth - TIDAL (Set access token) ─── post-response script ───────────────
// Parses the OAuth2 token response and writes tidal_access_token + expiry.
// Works for both client_credentials and refresh_token grant types.
pm.test("TIDAL auth response has access_token", function () {
console.error("[TIDAL Auth] Failed to parse response JSON:", e.message);
console.error("[TIDAL Auth] No access_token in response. Body:", JSON.stringify(json));
console.log("[TIDAL Auth] Token saved. Expires in", expiresIn, "seconds (with 30s buffer).");

request.description:
Set `tidal_access_token` in the environment manually (secret).

This request exists to make the workflow explicit and runnable in Collection Runner (it will fail fast if the token is missing).

This is a GET request and it is used to "get" data from an endpoint. There is no request body for a GET request, but you can use query parameters to help specify the resource you want data on (e.g., in this request, we have `id=1`).

A successful GET response will have a `200 OK` status, and should include some kind of response body - for example, HTML web content or JSON data.

This is a GET request and it is used to "get" data from an endpoint. There is no request body for a GET request, but you can use query parameters to help specify the resource you want data on (e.g., in this request, we have `id=1`).

A successful GET response will have a `200 OK` status, and should include some kind of response body - for example, HTML web content or JSON data.

This is a POST request, submitting data to an API via the request body. This request submits JSON data, and the data is reflected in the response.

A successful POST request typically returns a `200 OK` or `201 Created` response code.

This is a POST request, submitting data to an API via the request body. This request submits JSON data, and the data is reflected in the response.

A successful POST request typically returns a `200 OK` or `201 Created` response code.

exec: // Only meaningful after both primary fetch+parse steps succeed
// Guard: both providers must have succeeded
'Compare skipped — provider fetch incomplete. TIDAL: ' + tidalStatus + ' | Beatport: ' + beatportMatchStatus);
const isrcMatch    = tidalIsrc && beatportIsrc && tidalIsrc === beatportIsrc;
const titleMatch   = tidalTitle && bpTitle && tidalTitle === bpTitle;
const artistMatch  = tidalArtist && bpArtist && tidalArtist === bpArtist;
if (!isrcMatch)  notes.push('ISRC mismatch: TIDAL=' + tidalIsrc + ' BP=' + beatportIsrc);
if (!titleMatch) notes.push('Title mismatch: TIDAL="' + tidalTitle + '" BP="' + bpTitle + '"');
if (!artistMatch) notes.push('Artist mismatch: TIDAL="' + tidalArtist + '" BP="' + bpArtist + '"');
if (!durMatch && tidalDur > 0 && bpDur > 0) notes.push('Duration diff: ' + durDiff + 'ms');

request.description:
Compares canonicalized TIDAL vs Beatport values and sets per-field comparison status.

Writes:
- comparison_status
- operator_summary (human-readable JSON)

Does not dedupe; retains both provider values + provenance.

exec: // Only meaningful after both primary fetch+parse steps succeed
// Guard: both providers must have succeeded
'Compare skipped — provider fetch incomplete. TIDAL: ' + tidalStatus + ' | Beatport: ' + beatportMatchStatus);
const isrcMatch    = tidalIsrc && beatportIsrc && tidalIsrc === beatportIsrc;
const titleMatch   = tidalTitle && bpTitle && tidalTitle === bpTitle;
const artistMatch  = tidalArtist && bpArtist && tidalArtist === bpArtist;
if (!isrcMatch)  notes.push('ISRC mismatch: TIDAL=' + tidalIsrc + ' BP=' + beatportIsrc);
if (!titleMatch) notes.push('Title mismatch: TIDAL="' + tidalTitle + '" BP="' + bpTitle + '"');
if (!artistMatch) notes.push('Artist mismatch: TIDAL="' + tidalArtist + '" BP="' + bpArtist + '"');
if (!durMatch && tidalDur > 0 && bpDur > 0) notes.push('Duration diff: ' + durDiff + 'ms');

request.description:
Compares canonicalized TIDAL vs Beatport values and sets per-field comparison status.

Writes:
- comparison_status
- operator_summary (human-readable JSON)

Does not dedupe; retains both provider values + provenance.

item.name: Fallback - Beatport Search by Query (tertiary, explicit trigger only)

exec: // EXPLICIT OPERATOR TRIGGER ONLY — not part of primary workflow.
// Fails loudly on 0 or >1 results. Only sets canonical vars
// if exactly one result AND its ISRC matches lookup_isrc.
// Warn loudly that this is a fallback path
console.warn('[BP FALLBACK] Text search is inherently ambiguous. Validate results carefully.');
// Locate results array — handle both {results:[]} and {tracks:{results:[]}} shapes
pm.test('FAIL: Cannot locate results array — schema drift', () => {
pm.expect.fail('Schema drift: no results array found. Do not proceed.');
`AMBIGUOUS: search returned ${count} result(s). Cannot safely identify track. Do not guess.`);
console.error('[BP FALLBACK] No results for query:', pm.environment.get('lookup_query'));
console.error('[BP FALLBACK] Ambiguous:', count, 'results. Refusing to set canonical vars.');
// Exactly one result — still must validate ISRC
`ISRC mismatch: got ${track.isrc}, expected ${expectedIsrc}. Refusing to set canonical vars.`);
console.error('[BP FALLBACK] ISRC mismatch. Not setting canonical vars.');
console.log('[BP FALLBACK] Matched via search:', track.name, '| ISRC:', track.isrc);

request.description: FALLBACK — Beatport text search. Must only be run when explicitly triggered by the operator (not part of the primary workflow). Results are inherently ambiguous; the test script enforces loud failure on 0 or >1 results and will not set canonical vars unless exactly one result is returned and its ISRC matches lookup_isrc.
